Package org.eventb.core.sc.state

Interface IAbstractEventInfo

All Superinterfaces:
Comparable<IAbstractEventInfo>, IConvergenceInfo, ISCState, IState
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractEventInfo
public interface IAbstractEventInfo extends IConvergenceInfo, Comparable<IAbstractEventInfo>
This state (sub-) component provides access to information about an abstract event. It is only accessible by way of IAbstractEventTable.

In comparisons only event labels are considered, i.e., two abstract event infos are considered equal if their labels are equal.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IAbstractEventTable

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<Assignment>
    getActions()
    Returns the array of parsed and type-checked assignments corresponding to the actions of the corresponding event.
    ISCEvent
    getEvent()
    Returns a handle to the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info.
    String
    getEventLabel()
    Returns the label of the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info.
    List<Predicate>
    getGuards()
    Returns the array of parsed and type-checked predicates corresponding to the guards of the corresponding event.
    List<IConcreteEventInfo>
    getMergers()
    Returns the list events that are supposed to merge this abstract event with another.
    FreeIdentifier
    getParameter(String name)
    Returns a typed free parameter with the specified name contained in this abstract event info, or null if no parameter with this name is contained in this abstract event info.
    List<FreeIdentifier>
    getParameters()
    Returns the array of typed free parameters contained in this abstract event info.
    boolean
    getRefined()
    Tells if the handled event was refined.
    IRefinesMachine
    getRefinesMachine()
    Returns the refines machine clause that links the concrete machine to the abstract machine that contains the corresponding event.
    List<IConcreteEventInfo>
    getSplitters()
    Returns the list events that are supposed to split this abstract event.
    boolean
    isClosed()
    Tells whether the corresponding event is closed, i.e., contains a guard which is the predicate false (⊥).
    void
    setRefined()
    Sets the boolean value refined indicating that the handled event is refined.

  • Field Details

    • STATE_TYPE

      static final IStateType<IAbstractEventInfo> STATE_TYPE

  • Method Details

    • getEvent

      ISCEvent getEvent()
      Returns a handle to the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info.
      Returns:
      a handle to the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info

    • getEventLabel

      String getEventLabel()
      Returns the label of the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info.

      Calling this method should be prefered over getEvent().getLabel() because it is more efficient.

      Returns:
      the label of the SC event corresponding to this abstract event info

    • getParameter

      FreeIdentifier getParameter(String name) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns a typed free parameter with the specified name contained in this abstract event info, or null if no parameter with this name is contained in this abstract event info.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the free identifier to look up
      Returns:
      the corresponding typed free identifier, or null if none
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if state is not immutable

    • getParameters

      List<FreeIdentifier> getParameters() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the array of typed free parameters contained in this abstract event info.

      The free identifiers correspond to event parameters.

      Returns:
      the array of typed free identifiers contained in this abstract event info
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if state is not immutable

    • getGuards

      List<Predicate> getGuards() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the array of parsed and type-checked predicates corresponding to the guards of the corresponding event.
      Returns:
      the array of parsed and type-checked predicates corresponding to the guards
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if state is not immutable of the corresponding event

    • isClosed

      boolean isClosed() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Tells whether the corresponding event is closed, i.e., contains a guard which is the predicate false (⊥).
      Returns:
      whether the corresponding event is closed
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Since:
      2.2

    • getActions

      List<Assignment> getActions() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the array of parsed and type-checked assignments corresponding to the actions of the corresponding event.
      Returns:
      the array of parsed and type-checked assignments corresponding to the actions
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if state is not immutable of the corresponding event

    • getRefinesMachine

      IRefinesMachine getRefinesMachine()
      Returns the refines machine clause that links the concrete machine to the abstract machine that contains the corresponding event.
      Returns:
      the refines clause through which this event is introduced
      Since:
      2.2

    • getMergers

      List<IConcreteEventInfo> getMergers()
      Returns the list events that are supposed to merge this abstract event with another.
      Returns:
      the list events that are supposed to merge this abstract event with another

    • getSplitters

      List<IConcreteEventInfo> getSplitters()
      Returns the list events that are supposed to split this abstract event.
      Returns:
      the list events that are supposed to merge this abstract event

    • setRefined

      void setRefined()
      Sets the boolean value refined indicating that the handled event is refined.
      Since:
      2.0

    • getRefined

      boolean getRefined()
      Tells if the handled event was refined.
      Returns:
      true if the handled was refined by a concrete event, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2.0
